tasks.subject: [shortcut-discoverability] [P1] Add shared isMac constant and replace all duplicated definitions
tasks.description:
Add a shared `isMac` constant to the existing platform utilities file and replace all 6 duplicated inline definitions across the codebase.

## Step 1: Add isMac to platform.ts

File: `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/platform.ts`

Add the following export after the existing `PlatformAdapter` code:

```typescript
/** Whether the current platform is macOS/iOS (used for shortcut display). */
export const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);
```

## Step 2: Add barrel export

File: `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/index.ts`

Update the platform re-export line to include `isMac`:

```typescript
export { getPlatform, setPlatformAdapter, isMac, type PlatformAdapter } from './platform';
```

## Step 3: Replace all 6 duplicated isMac definitions

Each file below has a local `const isMac = ...` that must be removed and replaced with a shared import.

### 3a. `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/AgentSidebar.tsx`
- Remove: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);` (line 172)
- Add to existing shared/lib import: `isMac`
- The file already imports from `@/layers/shared/lib` (line 3: `import { cn, groupSessionsByTime, TIMING } from '@/layers/shared/lib';`). Add `isMac` to that import.

### 3b. `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/ui/CommandPaletteTrigger.tsx`
- Remove: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);` (around line 7)
- Add: `import { isMac } from '@/layers/shared/lib';`

### 3c. `apps/client/src/App.tsx`
- Remove: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);` (line 102)
- Add `isMac` to an import from `@/layers/shared/lib`. The file does not currently import from shared/lib, so add: `import { isMac } from '@/layers/shared/lib';`

### 3d. `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SidebarTabRow.tsx`
- Remove: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);` (line 41)
- Add: `import { isMac } from '@/layers/shared/lib';`

### 3e. `apps/client/src/layers/features/command-palette/ui/PaletteFooter.tsx`
- Remove: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.platform?.includes('Mac');` (line 21) — note this uses a different detection pattern (`.includes('Mac')` vs regex). Standardize on the regex pattern from the shared constant.
- Add: `import { isMac } from '@/layers/shared/lib';`

### 3f. `apps/client/src/layers/features/command-palette/ui/AgentSubMenu.tsx`
- Remove: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.platform?.includes('Mac');` (line 38) — same non-standard pattern as PaletteFooter.
- Add: `import { isMac } from '@/layers/shared/lib';`

## Important Notes

- The `isMac` constant is module-level (not inside a component), so it evaluates once at import time. This matches the existing pattern where each file defines `const isMac` at module scope.
- In `AgentSidebar.tsx`, the `isMac` is currently defined inside the component function body (line 172). Moving to a module-level import is fine since the value never changes during the app lifecycle.
- In `SidebarTabRow.tsx`, the `isMac` is inside the component body (line 41). Same consideration applies.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/platform.ts` exports `isMac` as a named constant
- [ ] `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/index.ts` re-exports `isMac`
- [ ] All 6 files (`AgentSidebar.tsx`, `CommandPaletteTrigger.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `SidebarTabRow.tsx`, `PaletteFooter.tsx`, `AgentSubMenu.tsx`) use the shared import instead of local definitions
- [ ] No remaining `const isMac` definitions in the codebase (verify with grep)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] Existing behavior is unchanged (keyboard shortcuts display correctly on Mac and non-Mac)
tasks.activeForm: Adding shared isMac constant and replacing duplicated definitions

tasks.subject: [shortcut-discoverability] [P1] Add shortcutsPanelOpen state to Zustand app-store
tasks.description:
Add the `shortcutsPanelOpen` transient dialog state to the Zustand app-store, following the exact same pattern as `globalPaletteOpen`.

## Step 1: Update AppState interface

File: `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store.ts`

Add the following three properties to the `AppState` interface, after the `clearGlobalPaletteInitialSearch` line (around line 74):

```typescript
shortcutsPanelOpen: boolean;
setShortcutsPanelOpen: (open: boolean) => void;
toggleShortcutsPanel: () => void;
```

## Step 2: Update store implementation

In the `create<AppState>()` call, add the implementation after the `clearGlobalPaletteInitialSearch` implementation (around line 239):

```typescript
shortcutsPanelOpen: false,
setShortcutsPanelOpen: (open) => set({ shortcutsPanelOpen: open }),
toggleShortcutsPanel: () => set((s) => ({ shortcutsPanelOpen: !s.shortcutsPanelOpen })),
```

This follows the exact pattern of `globalPaletteOpen`/`setGlobalPaletteOpen`/`toggleGlobalPalette`.

Note: This is transient dialog state (not persisted to localStorage), matching the existing pattern for `settingsOpen`, `pulseOpen`, `relayOpen`, `meshOpen`, `pickerOpen`, `agentDialogOpen`, and `globalPaletteOpen`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AppState` interface includes `shortcutsPanelOpen`, `setShortcutsPanelOpen`, and `toggleShortcutsPanel`
- [ ] Store implementation initializes `shortcutsPanelOpen` to `false`
- [ ] `toggleShortcutsPanel` flips the boolean (same pattern as `toggleGlobalPalette`)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
tasks.activeForm: Adding shortcutsPanelOpen state to Zustand app-store

tasks.subject: [shortcut-discoverability] [P2] Replace New Session button tooltip with inline Kbd hint
tasks.description: Modify the 'New session' button in `AgentSidebar.tsx` to show the keyboard shortcut inline on hover instead of in a tooltip. ## File: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/AgentSidebar.tsx` ### Step 1: Update imports The file currently imports (lines 1-17): ```typescript import { useMemo, useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useContext } from 'react'; import { useAppStore, useIsMobile } from '@/layers/shared/model'; import { cn, groupSessionsByTime, TIMING } from '@/layers/shared/lib'; import { SidebarContent, SidebarContext, SidebarFooter, SidebarHeader, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarRail, Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent, Kbd, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Changes needed: - Add `formatShortcutKey, SHORTCUTS` to the `@/layers/shared/lib` import - Remove `Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent` from `@/layers/shared/ui` import (verify they are not used elsewhere in the file first; based on the current code, these are only used for the New Session button tooltip) - Keep `Kbd` in the `@/layers/shared/ui` import Updated imports: ```typescript import { cn, groupSessionsByTime, TIMING, formatShortcutKey, SHORTCUTS } from '@/layers/shared/lib'; import { SidebarContent, SidebarContext, SidebarFooter, SidebarHeader, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarRail, Kbd, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` ### Step 2: Remove local isMac Remove line 172: `const isMac = typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);` (Already handled by task 1.1, but mentioning here for completeness since this task changes the same area) ### Step 3: Replace the button JSX Replace the Tooltip-wrapped button (lines 179-193): ```tsx {/* BEFORE */} New session New session {isMac ? '\u21e7\u2318N' : 'Ctrl+Shift+N'} ``` With the inline hint pattern: ```tsx {/* AFTER */} New session {formatShortcutKey(SHORTCUTS.NEW_SESSION)} ``` Key changes: 1. Remove `Tooltip`/`TooltipTrigger`/`TooltipContent` wrapper entirely 2. Add `group` to button className (enables `group-hover` on children) 3. Change `justify-center` to `justify-between` (label left, kbd right) 4. Wrap icon+label in a `` container 5. Add `Kbd` with fade-in classes: `shrink-0 opacity-0 transition-opacity duration-150 group-hover:opacity-100` 6. Use `formatShortcutKey(SHORTCUTS.NEW_SESSION)` instead of manual platform ternary ### Behavior details: - The `Kbd` element is always in the DOM but invisible (`opacity-0`), preventing layout shift on hover - On group-hover, `Kbd` fades in over 150ms (`transition-opacity duration-150`) - `shrink-0` prevents the Kbd from being squished in flex layout - The existing `Kbd` component already has `pointer-events-none` and `hidden md:inline-flex` (hidden on mobile) - `group` on the button + `group-hover:opacity-100` on the Kbd is a standard Tailwind pattern ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Tooltip wrapper is completely removed from the New Session button - [ ] `Kbd` hint appears inline (right side of button) on hover with a 150ms opacity transition - [ ] No layout shift when hovering — button dimensions remain constant - [ ] `Kbd` displays platform-correct shortcut (e.g., `\u21e7\u2318N` on Mac, `CTRL+SHIFT+N` on Windows) - [ ] `Kbd` is hidden on mobile (inherent from the Kbd component's `hidden md:inline-flex`) - [ ] `Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent` imports are removed if not used elsewhere in the file - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes - [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
tasks.activeForm: Replacing New Session button tooltip with inline Kbd hint

tasks.subject: [shortcut-discoverability] [P3] Mount ShortcutsPanel and useShortcutsPanel in App.tsx
tasks.description:
Wire the shortcuts feature into the main application by mounting the hook and component in `App.tsx`.

## File: `apps/client/src/App.tsx`

### Step 1: Add import

Add the following import near the other feature imports (around line 8-11):

```typescript
import { ShortcutsPanel, useShortcutsPanel } from '@/layers/features/shortcuts';
```

### Step 2: Add hook call

Inside the `App` component function body, add the hook call. Place it near the other effect hooks (after `useDocumentTitle`, around line 60):

```typescript
useShortcutsPanel(); // Register ? key handler
```

### Step 3: Add component to JSX

Add `<ShortcutsPanel />` alongside `<CommandPaletteDialog />` in both render paths:

**Embedded mode** (around line 174):
```tsx
<CommandPaletteDialog />
<ShortcutsPanel />
<Toaster />
```

**Standalone mode** (around line 254):
```tsx
<DialogHost />
<CommandPaletteDialog />
<ShortcutsPanel />
<Toaster />
```

The `ShortcutsPanel` is placed after `CommandPaletteDialog` in both paths. Both are dialog components that render via portals, so their position in the JSX tree doesn't affect visual stacking — it's just for code organization consistency.

### Important Notes

- The `useShortcutsPanel()` hook must be called inside the `App` component (not conditionally), so the `?` key handler is always registered.
- The hook is called once at the top level, not inside the embedded/standalone branches.
- The `<ShortcutsPanel />` component must appear in BOTH the embedded and standalone JSX branches since they are separate return paths.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `useShortcutsPanel()` is called in the App component body
- [ ] `<ShortcutsPanel />` is rendered in both embedded and standalone modes
- [ ] Pressing `?` opens the shortcuts reference panel
- [ ] Pressing `?` again closes the panel (toggle behavior)
- [ ] Pressing `Escape` closes the panel (built into ResponsiveDialog)
- [ ] Clicking outside closes the panel (built into ResponsiveDialog)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
tasks.activeForm: Mounting ShortcutsPanel and useShortcutsPanel in App.tsx

tasks.subject: [shortcut-discoverability] [P4] Update keyboard-shortcuts.md with ? shortcut and registry documentation
tasks.description:
Update the contributing guide with the new `?` shortcut and document the centralized shortcuts registry.

## File: `contributing/keyboard-shortcuts.md`

### Step 1: Add ? shortcut to Navigation table

In the "Keyboard Shortcut Reference" > "Navigation" section (around line 78-85), add a new row to the table:

```markdown
### Navigation

| Key                            | Action               |
| ------------------------------ | -------------------- |
| `Cmd+K` / `Ctrl+K`            | Open command palette |
| `Cmd+B` / `Ctrl+B`            | Toggle sidebar (Shadcn built-in `SIDEBAR_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUT`) |
| `Cmd+Shift+N` / `Ctrl+Shift+N` | New session         |
| `?`                              | Open keyboard shortcuts panel |
```

### Step 2: Add Shortcut Registry section

Add a new section at the end of the file (before or after the "Adding Shortcuts for a New Interactive Tool" section):

```markdown
## Shortcut Registry

All shortcuts are defined in `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/shortcuts.ts` as a centralized `SHORTCUTS` constant. Adding a new shortcut to the registry automatically makes it appear in:

- The `?` shortcuts reference panel
- The command palette (if a feature item references it)
- Inline button hints (if the button uses `formatShortcutKey`)

The registry is the single source of truth. Do not define shortcut display strings inline.

### Adding a New Shortcut to the Registry

1. Add a new entry to the `SHORTCUTS` object in `shortcuts.ts` with `id`, `key`, `label`, and `group`
2. The `key` uses a normalized format: `mod+` for Cmd/Ctrl, `shift+`, `alt+`, `ctrl+`
3. The shortcut automatically appears in the `?` reference panel (grouped by `group`)
4. Use `formatShortcutKey(SHORTCUTS.YOUR_SHORTCUT)` in UI to display the platform-appropriate key string
5. Use `import { isMac } from '@/layers/shared/lib'` instead of inline `navigator.platform` checks
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Navigation table includes `?` shortcut row
- [ ] New "Shortcut Registry" section documents the centralized registry
- [ ] Section explains how to add new shortcuts
- [ ] References the correct file path (`apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/shortcuts.ts`)
- [ ] No broken markdown formatting

tasks.subject: [shortcut-discoverability] [P4] Run typecheck and lint, fix any issues
tasks.description:
Run the full type-check and lint passes across the monorepo and fix any issues introduced by the shortcut-discoverability feature.

## Commands to run

```bash
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
```

## Common issues to watch for

1. **Unused imports**: After removing `Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent` from `AgentSidebar.tsx`, ensure they aren't still imported. Same for removing `isMac` local definitions.

2. **Missing TSDoc**: The `eslint-plugin-jsdoc` rule requires TSDoc on exported functions. Ensure:
   - `formatShortcutKey` has TSDoc (included in the registry code)
   - `getShortcutsGrouped` has TSDoc (included in the registry code)
   - `useShortcutsPanel` has TSDoc (included in the hook code)
   - `ShortcutsPanel` has TSDoc (included in the component code)
   - Barrel `index.ts` has module-level TSDoc (included in the barrel code)

3. **Import ordering**: Prettier + ESLint may reorder imports. Run `pnpm format` if needed.

4. **FSD layer violations**: Verify no cross-layer violations:
   - `shared/lib/shortcuts.ts` only imports from `shared/lib/platform.ts` (same layer) — OK
   - `features/shortcuts/model/use-shortcuts-panel.ts` imports from `shared/model` — OK
   - `features/shortcuts/ui/ShortcutsPanel.tsx` imports from `shared/model`, `shared/ui`, `shared/lib` — OK
   - `App.tsx` imports from `features/shortcuts` — OK (App layer can import from any layer)

5. **Tailwind class ordering**: `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` sorts classes automatically. Run `pnpm format` to auto-fix.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes with zero errors (or only pre-existing warnings)
- [ ] `pnpm format` has been run to normalize formatting
- [ ] All tests pass: `pnpm test -- --run`
tasks.activeForm: Running typecheck and lint, fixing issues
